rows.requirement: Planned dates, duration, work, and progress validation
evidence: P5-003 implementation exists in packages/scheduling-engine/src/index.ts: validateSchedulePlan returns typed ScheduleValidationIssue entries for missing planned start/finish, missing planned work/progress, invalid date ranges, invalid planned work, and invalid progress on task-backed WBS nodes
Validation is non-mutating; unit test covers source input JSON remaining unchanged after validateSchedulePlan

Bug Hunt found one Important issue where early planned-field return hid dependency conflicts; fixed by collecting finish-to-start conflicts alongside planned-field issues when valid dates are available
External code review returned no Critical or Important findings
Structured Phase 5 E2E evidence E2E-042 now proves planned date/work/progress persistence, audit, reload, and invalid date-range rejection without mutation.

evidence: P5-004 implementation exists in packages/scheduling-engine/src/index.ts: Finish-to-Start dependency construction rejects self-links, unknown task-backed WBS endpoints, cross-tenant/project references, duplicate dependency keys, and dependency cycles
validateSchedulePlan now runs structural dependency validation before planned-field issue collection, so mixed-invalid plans cannot hide dependency cycles or malformed dependency endpoints
Successor-before-predecessor schedule conflicts remain typed ScheduleValidationIssue entries with code finish_to_start_conflict; no automatic date propagation or auto-rescheduling is introduced

Bug Hunt found no Critical or Important issues after review fix; requested code review found one Medium issue about mixed-invalid validation hiding structural dependency errors, fixed with assertScheduleDependencyStructure and a regression test
Mandatory Phase 5 E2E evidence E2E-043 now exists for P5-004 phase-exit verification
Structured Phase 5 E2E evidence E2E-043 now proves Finish-to-Start dependency persistence and typed conflict blocker behavior without partial mutation.

evidence: Implemented single draft baseline capture/update primitives in packages/scheduling-engine/src/index.ts.
Baseline snapshots capture visible task start, finish, derived duration, and progress from task-backed WBS nodes only.
Snapshots are tenant/project/schedulePlan scoped, defensive-copied, immutable after live plan drift, and deterministically ordered by WBS sortOrder/id.

Invalid baseline values, duplicate baseline task ids, mismatched capture tenant/project/schedulePlan references, incomplete captured task values, and duration/date mismatches are rejected before snapshot creation.
Bug Hunt fixed duplicate baseline task ids and duration/date mismatch acceptance; requested code review fixed top-level snapshot immutability and incomplete capture rows.
Implementation deliberately excludes multiple named baselines, approvals, earned value, retrospective analytics, API, UI, fixtures, and E2E.
Structured Phase 5 E2E evidence E2E-044 now proves baseline draft values remain stable after live schedule drift.
tests: RED: npm test -- packages/scheduling-engine -> exit 1 before implementation; 6 expected baseline tests failed.
GREEN: npm test -- packages/scheduling-engine -> exit 0; 2 test files, 24 tests passed after Bug Hunt and code review fixes.
npm run test:e2e:phase -- --phase=5 exit 0; E2E-040..044 passed; metadata test-results/kiss-pm-e2e-last-run.json
rows.cleanup: No runtime cleanup needed for pure scheduling-engine value objects.

evidence: Implemented Schedule/Gantt API endpoints in apps/api/src/app.ts: GET schedule, POST schedule canonical task, PATCH schedule fields, POST Finish-to-Start dependency, POST baseline draft capture, and GET schedule audit/action evidence.
Added apps/api/src/phase5Runtime.ts for deterministic in-memory schedule state, baseline snapshots, validation issue readback, and action execution logs without UI/network/domain mutation shortcuts.
Extended project-core with updateTaskPlanningFields so schedule updates preserve canonical Task identity and update Task.dueDate/plannedWorkHours instead of creating a Gantt-only task entity.
State-changing schedule commands require backend permission checks and append both audit events and action execution evidence.
Invalid Finish-to-Start dependency commands return safe precondition_failed DTOs with typed validationIssues and do not persist partial dependencies.
Bug Hunt added regression coverage proving invalid schedule patch and unknown dependency endpoint commands reject before partial mutation.

Requested code review found Important undeclared scheduling-engine dependency and schedule audit leakage across same-tenant project entity-id collisions; fixed by declaring @kiss-pm/scheduling-engine in apps/api/package.json/package-lock.json and filtering schedule audit readback by action correlation id.
Requested code review found Medium duplicate dependency-id ambiguity; fixed by rejecting duplicate dependency ids in phase5Runtime before storage.
Structured Phase 5 E2E evidence E2E-040..044 now proves schedule API read/write, permissions, tenant isolation, audit/action evidence, dependency validation, and baseline behavior.

evidence: Implementation present: Russian Gantt workspace is rendered from schedule API/read model in apps/web with WBS rows, planned start/finish, duration, planned work, progress, baseline column, validation warnings, loading/empty/denied/error states, project surface entrypoint, and portfolio/project-id entry panel.

Browser smoke on 2026-05-16: local API+Vite at 127.0.0.1 showed status Гантт загружен, stage/task WBS rows, Шкала timeline, Базовый план header, Russian audit labels, and Открыть Гантт проекта project entrypoint.

Bug Hunt + requested code review Important findings resolved: real project entrypoint added, loading/error no longer collapse into empty state, React no longer performs schedule date formulas and renders a schedule Gantt view projection.
Structured Phase 5 E2E evidence E2E-040 now proves Russian Gantt entrypoint/workspace loading, WBS rows, tenant isolation, and read-only denial.

rows.requirement: Gantt task creation and inline schedule edits
evidence: Implementation present in apps/web: Gantt command panel creates canonical schedule tasks through P5-006 POST /schedule/tasks, edits planned start/finish/work/progress through PATCH /schedule/tasks/:taskId, creates Finish-to-Start dependencies through POST /schedule/dependencies, and captures baseline draft through POST /schedule/baseline.
UI command results are not accepted as local-only truth: after each command Gantt reloads the active project schedule/audit from API readback.
Russian denied and typed validation issue states are visible for schedule commands; read-only users do not see mutation buttons.
Baseline values are rendered separately from live schedule fields; browser smoke verified live date/progress edit after baseline capture left baseline values unchanged.

Browser smoke on 2026-05-16 against local Vite+API: project entrypoint refreshed same project, two Gantt-created canonical tasks persisted, FS dependency persisted, baseline captured, inline edit persisted after API reload, audit/action evidence rendered, and no new console errors appeared after reload.

Same canonical task identity is partially proven by P5-006 API integration and existing Phase 4 My Tasks/Kanban projections; exact Gantt-created task propagation across My Tasks/Kanban remains a P5-009 E2E fixture/readback responsibility because current web surfaces use independent clients and no shared query invalidation.

Requested code review Important findings resolved: read-only users no longer get inline schedule draft inputs, and validation issue labels are localized to Russian by issue code while preserving typed codes.
Structured Phase 5 E2E evidence E2E-041..044 now proves Gantt task create, inline schedule edit, FS dependency command, baseline capture, reload persistence, and cross-view canonical task readback.
